Empires & Puzzles Fawn Review: The 3-Star Dodgeball Champion

Is Fawn the best 3-star support hero? This review breaks down her 'Evading Dash' skill, which brings 5-star mechanics like team-wide dodge to the rare arena. Discover why she's a nightmare in Buff Booster tournaments.
Fawn: The Little Engine That Could... Dodge

Meet Fawn, the enthusiastic squire from the Clash of Knights event who seemingly wandered into the wrong arena but decided to stay and ruin everyone's day anyway. Released in late 2023, she brings a mechanic typically reserved for the elite 5-star club—
. While she might look like she's just happy to be here, don't let that smile fool you; she's essentially a pocket-sized Costume Kadilen, ready to make your opponent's snipers miss every single shot.
Fawn's artwork perfectly captures her "Devoted Squire" title. She's bright-eyed, energetic, and sporting armor that looks just a

too clean for actual combat. The antler motif ties her neatly into the Stag family, and her expression screams, "I'm going to dodge your axe and look adorable doing it!" It’s a refreshing change from the usual grimacing warriors covered in spikes and skulls.

is quite low, even by older standards, but her
is absolutely massive for a 3-star hero.
To put this in perspective, newer "power creep" 3-star heroes like

, meaning Fawn still holds the crown for durability. However, her Defense stat (460) is average, meaning she relies heavily on that health pool and her special skill to survive. She is built to last, not to blast.
This skill is the reason you level Fawn.
is a game-changer in the Rare rarity.
This is massive. In 3-star tournaments, where dispellers can be scarce or slow, a 45% chance to completely negate damage and status ailments for the

It's not a percentage counterattack, but a flat 80 damage per dodge. In 3-star battles where health pools are smaller (roughly 800-1200 HP), chipping away 80 HP for every miss adds up surprisingly fast.

The "small amount of mana" (roughly 0.4 - 0.5 tiles per turn) might seem negligible, but over 3 turns, it acts as a passive mana generator that can help slower allies charge up or break tile breakpoints.

. With a level 11 mana troop (available in some events but not 3-star tournaments) or family bonuses, she can fire in 7 tiles. In 3-star Rush tournaments, she becomes Very Fast (6.5 tiles), making her protection almost immediate.
A 45% dodge chance is effectively a 45% damage reduction over time, but better—because it also avoids status ailments like defense down or mindless attack.
While she doesn't heal directly, her passive (see below) provides self-sustain.
Her passive heals her for 5% HP (approx. 50-60 HP) every time she receives a buff. Since her skill gives
buffs (Dodge and Mana Gen), she heals herself for 10% instantly upon firing, plus any other buffs from allies.
Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities

bonus is fantastic for a support hero. It essentially makes her immune to Attack Down ailments. Instead of hitting like a wet noodle, she gets a massive attack buff (+40% to +70%). While her base attack is low, this ensures her tile damage remains relevant if the enemy tries to weaken her.
means Fawn loves Buff Booster tournaments. Pair her with other buffers (like Grevle or Faiez), and she becomes incredibly difficult to kill.

is the perfect Aether Power for her. Since she is a support hero who needs to stay alive to keep the dodge active, a flat defense boost at the start of the battle helps her survive the opening turns before she can cast her skill.
Upon release, Fawn was immediately identified as a "mini-C.Kadilen." She brought a mechanic previously locked behind 5-star paywalls to the masses.
While her attack stat is laughable compared to modern 3-star nukers like

, her utility has not aged a day. Dodge is a mechanic that defies power creep—avoiding 1000 damage is just as valuable as avoiding 500 damage. Her high HP base keeps her relevant even as damage numbers inflate.
Fawn remains a top-tier pick for 3-star Tournaments, specifically
. She is less effective in Bloody Battle (where her self-heal passive is useless) but the dodge is still potent.
Fawn is not there to deal damage; she is there to annoy the opponent into submission.

You have Overheal, Defense Up, Attack Up, Dodge, and Mana Gen. The sheer number of buffs makes your team hit incredibly hard due to the tournament rule, while being nearly impossible to kill.
Fawn protects the team, Bauchan cuts mana, and Buster increases damage taken. If they hit you, they miss. If they don't, they get crushed.

If you pull Fawn, lock her and level her immediately. She will carry you through difficult map stages and rare quests by simply keeping your team alive. She is essentially a cheat code for early-game survival.

You might think 3-stars are just for tournaments, and you're right—but winning tournaments yields Aethers. Fawn is a staple for top 1% finishes in 3-star Buff Booster. If you don't have her leveled and emblemed, you are playing at a disadvantage. Limit break her for the extra stats; she is worth the resources.
The community recognized Fawn's potential immediately.
“She’s an absolute BEAST. Just finished her and currently wrecking.”
— A satisfied player shortly after release.
. I have a tough time facing her on defense teams. When I see Basil... I know it’s a win.”
— A common sentiment regarding the power shift in red 3-star tanks.
— The general consensus for the rare arena.

Fawn is a 5-star hero trapped in a 3-star body. She brings elite-level damage mitigation to a rarity tier that usually consists of "hit hard" or "heal simple." While her attack stat suggests she's harmless, her ability to make an entire enemy team waste their specials into thin air makes her one of the most frustrating—and valuable—heroes in the rare roster.
For tournament players, absolutely. For everyone else, she's the cutest insurance policy you'll ever own.
A Must-Have for the avid 3-Star Tournament player.
A comprehensive review of Fawn, the 3-star Fire hero from the Stag family. We analyze her stats, unique dodge skill, and viability in competitive 3-star tournaments.
